title: iris stenophylla subsp. allisonii
intro: Iris stenophylla subsp. allisonii, also known as the Allison's iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Iris stenophylla subsp. allisonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water or ethanol, which can be used to treat various health issues.
